clean_output:
summary: The article keeps the main performance tension visible: compact oracle summaries beat both no experience and full trajectory reuse, while non-oracle summaries underperform the baseline.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: Full prior experience did not materially outperform the no-experience baseline and performed far worse than compact oracle summaries, which conflicts with any simple expectation that more reused context should help more.
    related_claim_texts:
      - In the reported results, oracle summary reuse outperformed both no-experience and oracle full-experience reuse, reaching 34.34% resolution versus 26.26% for the baseline and 27.27% for full trajectory reuse.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-011
      - fact-012
      - fact-013
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-11
      - evidence-12
      - evidence-13
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: The article should keep visible that more prior context was not better in this benchmark; the observed advantage belongs to short oracle summaries, not to raw experience volume.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: Summary reuse is presented as beneficial in the best-case oracle setting, but free summary reuse performed worse than doing nothing and produced the most patch application failures, creating tension between the promise of compressed context and its practical reliability.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The benchmark results suggest that summary quality matters, because free summary reuse performed worse than the no-experience baseline and produced the most patch application failures.
      - In the reported results, oracle summary reuse outperformed both no-experience and oracle full-experience reuse, reaching 34.34% resolution versus 26.26% for the baseline and 27.27% for full trajectory reuse.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-012
      - fact-014
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-12
      - evidence-14
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: The article should state plainly that compressed context is not inherently helpful; the gains depend on summary quality, and poor summaries can actively degrade outcomes.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: Oracle summary reuse was simultaneously the most accurate, fastest, and cheapest condition, but the evidence does not separate whether the gain comes mainly from better information selection, lower context-processing burden, or both.
    related_claim_texts:
      - In the reported results, oracle summary reuse outperformed both no-experience and oracle full-experience reuse, reaching 34.34% resolution versus 26.26% for the baseline and 27.27% for full trajectory reuse.
      - SWE-ContextBench evaluates coding agents not just on task resolution but also on runtime and monetary cost using test-based outcome checks on related tasks.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-002
      - fact-012
      - fact-013
      - fact-015
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-02
      - evidence-12
      - evidence-13
      - evidence-15
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: The article should keep visible that the benchmark shows a better joint operating point for oracle summaries, but does not isolate the causal contribution of summary quality versus shorter context length and lower processing cost.
